task_completion.description: Verify the agent inspects the provided auditd log samples.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the agent reading the contents of '/home/ga/data/audit_staging_sample.log' using commands like 'cat', 'less', or a text editor.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent accesses the correct Wazuh manager container.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the execution of a command like 'docker exec -it wazuh-wazuh.manager-1 bash' or similar to access the container shell.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent modifies the local_rules.xml file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a text editor (e.g., nano, vi) open for '/var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml' or echo/sed commands appending to this specific file.

task_completion.description: Verify the custom rule has the correct ID, level, and match conditions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the rule block in the terminal or editor containing '<rule id="100250" level="10">' and match/regex tags containing the strings 'tar' and 'sensitive_project'.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent tests the rule against the malicious log sample.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the agent running '/var/ossec/bin/wazuh-logtest' (or similar) and pasting the malicious log sample, with the output showing that rule 100250 successfully fired in Phase 3.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent tests the rule against the benign log sample to ensure it is ignored.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the agent running '/var/ossec/bin/wazuh-logtest' with the benign log sample, with the output showing that rule 100250 did NOT fire.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually interacted with the Docker container.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal prompt should reflect the container environment (e.g., 'root@...' inside the container) or commands should be explicitly routed through 'docker exec'. The agent should not just be editing a local mock file.

integrity.description: Ensure the wazuh-logtest output is genuine and not spoofed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The logtest output should display the standard Wazuh testing format, including 'Phase 1: Completed pre-decoding', 'Phase 2: Completed decoding', and 'Phase 3: Completed filtering (rules)', rather than just a printed success message.

integrity.description: Ensure the rule was added within a valid XML structure.
integrity.visual_evidence: The new rule should be enclosed within a valid '<group>' tag block in the local_rules.xml file, not just appended loosely at the end of the file outside the root XML tags.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The custom rule must have ID 100250 and level 10. It must contain match strings for 'tar' and 'sensitive_project'. The malicious log to test is: 'type=EXECVE msg=audit(1698234001.123:101): argc=4 a0="tar" a1="-czf" a2="/tmp/backup_sensitive.tar.gz" a3="/opt/sensitive_project"'. The benign log is: 'type=EXECVE msg=audit(1698234055.456:102): argc=4 a0="tar" a1="-czf" a2="/tmp/my_docs.tar.gz" a3="/home/user/documents"'.
